markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

Steady US production near 13.5 million bpd, high refinery utilization at 89.6%, and reduced imports have accelerated recent stockpile draws. Traders monitor weekly EIA reports, including the next on May 6 covering the week ending May 1, alongside potential Defense Production Act measures to ramp output, as seasonal demand and export trends could sustain declines through the June 5 measurement.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 27°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the razor-thin spread in latest short-range forecast models, with ECMWF and GFS ensembles centering Moscow's May 5 diurnal high temperature at 24-25°C amid a persistent high-pressure ridge over European Russia delivering above-normal spring warmth (historical May average ~18°C). Recent observations from May 3-4, including today's highs near 24°C under clear skies and light southerly winds, have nudged some models upward from earlier 23°C projections, tightening the 24°C (41%) vs. 25°C (40.5%) odds. Differentiating factors include subtle variations in boundary-layer mixing and potential afternoon cumulus development, which could cap heating at 24°C or allow a 25°C peak with sustained insolation. New model runs overnight and Vnukovo Airport METARs will refine this uncertainty before resolution.

markets.question: Wadi Degla SC vs. Kahrabaa Ismailia FC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Egypt Egyptian Premier League game between Wadi Degla SC and Kahrabaa Ismailia FC, scheduled for May 4 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Wadi Degla SC and Kahrabaa Ismailia FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efa.com.eg. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Egyptian Premier League relegation round, Wadi Degla SC sit second in the group standings after a gritty 0-0 draw away at Modern Sport on April 4, extending their strong defensive run with four clean sheets in their last six matches. markets.question: Will Pablo Lopez win the 2026 AL Cy Young Award?

eventMetadata.context_description: Cam Schlittler's elite early-season command, boasting a 1.51 ERA, MLB-best 0.74 WHIP, and 49 strikeouts across eight starts for the Yankees, has traders pricing him at 32% implied probability for the 2026 AL Cy Young after recent scoreless gems, including seven innings versus Seattle. Teammate Max Fried trails at 21.1% on seven quality starts with a 2.09 ERA, 0.80 WHIP, and 47.1 innings, capitalizing on his Opening Day role and consistent dominance in New York's deep rotation. Angels righty Jose Soriano surges to 12.4% behind a historic 0.24 ERA and 5-0 mark over 37.2 frames, echoing Jered Weaver's 2011 start, while Dylan Cease (10.5%) and Bryan Woo (8.7%) benefit from sub-3.00 ERAs and high strikeout rates amid favorable matchups.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' single-lap dominance, securing all poles through Japan with Kimi Antonelli and George Russell, has propelled them to the constructors' lead at 135 points after Miami, yet trader consensus prices every team near even at 49.5% for Canadian Grand Prix constructor pole position amid the 2026 regs' parity push. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's razor-thin margins, punishing kerbs, and Wall of Champions chicane amplify unpredictability, favoring clean laps over raw pace as track evolution and clean air prove decisive. Ferrari trails closely at 90 points with strong Q3 threats from Leclerc and Hamilton, while midfield risers like Haas and Alpine eye upgrades; newcomers Audi Revolut and Cadillac lag but could surprise in low-downforce setup.

events.title: Mirassol FC vs. Associação Chapecoense de Futebol
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Mirassol FC and Associação Chapecoense de Futebol.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mirassol's unblemished head-to-head record—four straight 1-0 wins over Chapecoense, including both Serie B clashes last year—combined with home advantage at Estádio José Maria de Campos Maia, anchors trader consensus at 65% implied probability for a home win amid both teams' struggles in the early Serie A relegation scrap. Mirassol sit 18th with a 1-3-6 record, hampered by recent losses like 0-1 to Coritiba and 3-2 at Botafogo, while bottom-placed Chapecoense (20th, 1-5-6 form) have drawn heavily but leaked goals in defeats such as 1-4 to Botafogo. Lingering injuries, including Mirassol's Igor Carius (thigh, early May question mark) and Chapecoense's cruciate ligament absences (Mancha, Bruno Matias), temper expectations, yet historical low-scoring dominance favors Mirassol's defensive edge in this table clash.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, a solidly Democratic North Shore Chicago suburb encompassing Evanston and Skokie, favors trader consensus on Democratic nominee Daniel Biss at 92.5% implied probability following his March 17 primary victory over 15 rivals in the open seat race triggered by Rep. Jan Schakowsky's 2025 retirement announcement. Biss, a former state senator and Evanston mayor with strong local fundraising exceeding $250,000 post-primary, faces low-profile Republican John Elleson in the November 3 general election. Historical margins exceeding 30 points for Democrats, absent competitive GOP spending or national midterm wave dynamics, underpin the pricing; upheaval would require a Biss scandal, surged Republican turnout, or unforeseen external shocks like endorsement shifts.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

Teammate George Russell, consistent with 80 points and multiple podiums including a win, trails closely at 28.0% implied odds, benefiting from the W16's superior pace, reliability, and strategic edge over rivals. Lando Norris sits fourth at 51 points with 11.1% odds, while Max Verstappen and Charles Leclerc lag further back amid Red Bull and Ferrari's adaptation struggles to the active aero and power unit changes, highlighting Mercedes' frontrunning form after Australia, China, Japan, and Miami.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tigers shortstop Kevin McGonigle leads AL Rookie of the Year trader consensus at 38.5% implied probability, edging White Sox first baseman Munetaka Murakami's 36%, as both deliver standout early-season performances through 30 games. McGonigle tops AL rookies in batting average (.333), on-base percentage (.420), slugging (.518), hits, doubles, and triples, bolstered by a 12-game hit streak and 25 consecutive games reaching base, giving him a WAR edge around 2.0. Murakami counters with explosive power, tying the MLB home run lead at 11—including a three-run shot on May 1—and a 1.020 OPS despite a .256 average, fueling recent odds flips amid voter appeal for counting stats like homers and RBI. The bunched odds reflect traders' split on well-rounded contact skills versus headline-grabbing slugging in a deep rookie class, with momentum hinging on sustained form, injuries, and schedule strength.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Slovenia
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Robert Golob's Freedom Movement's narrow win in the March 22 parliamentary elections, which produced a hung parliament under Slovenia's proportional representation system, the incumbent prime minister conceded on April 20 that he could not secure a coalition majority in the 90-seat National Assembly. This shifted momentum to Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), the runner-up with 28 seats, as he leads center-right coalition negotiations with parties like New Slovenia (NSi) under Jernej Vrtovec. President Nataša Pirc Musar urged lawmakers on April 25 to nominate a viable candidate, while Janša recently emphasized conditions must be met to avoid a weak government. Traders price Janša at 89% implied probability, viewing his prior experience and ongoing talks as decisive, though deadlock could trigger new elections.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid's league-leading 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists) and point in every Edmonton Oilers win (41/41) have solidified his position as the Hart Trophy frontrunner at 54% implied probability among traders, capped by a late-season tear that reclaimed the scoring lead after a tight race. Nikita Kucherov trails closely at 32% with 130 points fueling Tampa Bay Lightning's 50 wins and playoff berth, while Nathan MacKinnon's elite 5-on-5 dominance (+58 goals-for/against differential in 54 Avalanche victories) holds him at 17%, underscoring Colorado's top Western seed. Rookie Macklin Celebrini's breakout 115 points with San Jose garner 5% support amid Calder buzz, but the top trio's consistent MVP-caliber impact on contending teams drives the market consensus post-regular season.

eventMetadata.context_description: With first rehearsals underway at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle ahead of the May 12-14 semi-finals and May 16 grand final, trader consensus for Eurovision 2026 top 10 finishers emphasizes strong national selection standouts like Finland's violin-driven "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius x Pete Parkkonen and Greece's edgy "Ferto," which dominate OGAE polls and Eurojury results for their jury appeal and staging potential. Host Austria auto-qualifies with Cosmó Tanzschein's "Tanzschein," joined by Big 5 nations France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and UK, while frontrunners Australia (Delta Goodrem's "Eclipse") and Denmark eye high placements amid pre-party buzz. Israel's entry faces boycott headwinds potentially skewing votes, but rehearsals this week—revealing full stagings—remain the key catalyst for momentum shifts in this skin-in-the-game market.

markets.question: Will Juan Branco win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Jordan Bardella tops trader consensus at 25.5% for the 2027 French presidential election, mirroring recent Ifop and Elabe polls where the National Rally leader dominates first-round voting intentions amid public demand for change after Emmanuel Macron's two-term limit. Édouard Philippe follows at 19.5%, gaining from March 2026 municipal election results—his Le Havre mayoral reelection and RN's failure to capture major cities—plus runoff polls projecting his victory over Bardella via center-right consolidation. A crowded field of over 30 candidates, undecided party primaries, and September 2026 senatorial elections sustain tightness; endorsements, scandals, or coalition signals could widen gaps before April 2027 voting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Lindsey Graham leads trader consensus at 83% implied probability for the South Carolina Republican Senate primary on June 9, driven by his massive fundraising edge—over $20 million raised—Trump endorsement, and Governor Henry McMaster's support as campaign chair, reinforcing incumbency advantages in a solidly red state. Challenger Mark Lynch holds 12% after Paul Dans, Project 2025 architect, dropped out on April 10 and endorsed him, consolidating anti-Graham sentiment amid early polls showing Graham at 43% to Lynch's 29%. Paul Dans and Thomas Murphy trail at negligible levels due to limited resources and name recognition, with voter turnout and base mobilization key uncertainties ahead of the contest.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 76.5% implied probability against a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the FOMC's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% for a third straight meeting amid stubbornly elevated inflation—March 2026 CPI at 3.3% year-over-year—and resilient economic activity with solid job gains. March dot plot projections show a median end-2026 funds rate of 3.4%, signaling hold or modest easing rather than tightening, though minutes revealed some officials' openness to hikes if war-related oil shocks persist. Key catalysts include April CPI data due May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where persistent inflation above 2% could test this no-hike sentiment.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Manila be 29°C or below on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ph/manila/RPLL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Highest temperature in Manila on May 5?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

eventMetadata.context_description: PAGASA's latest 5-day weather outlook for Port Area in Metro Manila forecasts a high temperature of 35°C on May 5 under partly cloudy skies with isolated rainshowers and light easterly winds, driving trader consensus to price 35°C at 55.5% implied probability as the leading outcome. This aligns with May's climatological peak during the dry season, when solar insolation maximizes daytime heating amid typical highs of 33–34°C, though urban heat island effects in Manila can push readings higher. Recent ENSO-neutral conditions support near-normal rainfall per PAGASA's April outlook, tempering extremes, yet 21.5% odds on 36°C reflect potential for clearer conditions boosting intensity, while 16.5% on 34°C accounts for shower-cooling risks. Updated observations and heat index reports expected throughout the day could refine these market-implied odds.

markets.question: Exact Score: Sevilla FC 1 - 1 Real Sociedad de Fútbol?
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between Sevilla FC and Real Sociedad de Fútbol, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Sevilla FC vs. Real Sociedad de Fútbol match originally scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sevilla's home advantage at Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán clashes with Real Sociedad's superior 8th-place standing against Sevilla's 17th in La Liga, fueling trader consensus on a tight exact-score market where top outcomes like 1-0, 2-1, and 1-1 share equal 49.5% implied probabilities. Recent form underscores the deadlock: both lost 2-0 and 1-0 respectively last weekend to Levante and Getafe, with Sevilla winless in four of five (four goals scored, leaky defense no clean sheet in seven) and Real Sociedad drawing high-scoring ties like 3-3 vs. Alavés. Real Sociedad's defensive woes deepen the uncertainty, missing key center-back Igor Zubeldia (hamstring, early May return doubtful), Álvaro Odriozola (cruciate out until November), Aritz Elustondo, and Gonçalo Guedes, offsetting Sevilla's absences of César Azpilicueta, Marcão, and suspended Isaac Romero in their even head-to-head history (13 Sevilla wins, 12 Real Sociedad, six draws). Mild 26°C weather favors open play.

eventMetadata.context_description: CA Platense enters as trader consensus slight favorite at 46% implied probability for their Copa Libertadores Group E home clash against Peñarol, buoyed by a 2-1 upset victory in the first leg at Montevideo that vaulted them to second in the standings behind Corinthians. Peñarol's recent domestic losses and mounting injury crisis—key doubts over Ferreira, Darias, and Ángulo, plus long-term absences like Leonardo Fernández (knee) and Nahuel Herrera (shoulder)—have eroded their away threat to 24%, while conserving players for this pivotal fixture underscores its stakes for top-two qualification. Platense plans a near-identical lineup to their winning XI, with only minor tweaks like Gómez for Amarfil, amplifying home form in a closely contested matchup where draw pricing at 30.5% reflects defensive resilience on both sides.

markets.question: Will White House post 160-179 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times White House (@WhiteHouse), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecasts from the Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) and global models like ECMWF and GFS position Istanbul's May 5 high temperature around 15°C, driving trader consensus with 39% implied probability on that outcome, followed by 16°C (28%) and 14°C (24%). Persistent northerly winds at 10-15 km/h usher cool air from the Black Sea region, combined with near-total cloud cover (90-98%), suppress daytime heating below the early-May climatological average of 18-19°C. May 4's observed high of 14°C amid drizzle and 65% precipitation chance reinforces this pattern, with model ensembles showing tight spread but minor upside risk from potential afternoon clearing. New evening forecast updates from NOAA and ECMWF could shift odds before resolution based on evolving upper-air trough dynamics.

markets.question: Will Le Mans FC achieve promotion from Ligue 2 to Ligue 1 for the 2026-27 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team achieves promotion from the French Ligue 2 to Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the current league season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Ligue 2, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by the Ligue 1 and Ligue 2.

If the 2025-26 Ligue 2 season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from Ligue 1 and Ligue 2. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Troyes clinched the Ligue 2 title and automatic promotion to Ligue 1 with a dominant 4-0 victory over Laval on matchday 33 (May 2), extending their lead to eight points atop the table after 33 games played. Le Mans holds second at 59 points following a 1-1 draw against Reims, but faces mounting pressure from Saint-Étienne and Red Star (both 57 points) after their recent slip-ups—Saint-Étienne's third straight loss, a 2-1 defeat at unbeaten-in-2026 Rodez (55 points), and Red Star's solid 3-1 win at Amiens. With five matches remaining until the May 8 finale, the battle for the second automatic spot and promotion playoffs (3rd-5th) intensifies, hinging on form, goal difference, and head-to-head ties.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party of Korea candidate Park Chan-dae commands trader consensus at 96% implied probability to win the June 3 Incheon mayoral election, driven by recent polls showing him leading incumbent People Power Party Mayor Yoo Jeong-bok by 13–25 points, including an Incheon Ilbo survey last week with Park at 48% versus Yoo's 35%. As a three-term Incheon lawmaker and close ally of President Lee Jae-myung, Park leverages ruling party momentum, solidified party nominations in March, and his April 22 candidacy launch emphasizing economic growth pledges like boosting average salaries to top-5 nationally. Yoo's third-term bid faces incumbency fatigue amid DPK's 49% local support versus PPP's 28%. Late scandals, gaffes, or a conservative turnout surge could challenge this lead ahead of the winner-take-all vote.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue commands 83% trader consensus as the Massachusetts Republican gubernatorial primary frontrunner after securing the party endorsement with 70% of delegate votes at the April 26 state convention in Worcester, where Brian Shortsleeve narrowly qualified for the September 1 ballot at 15.5% and Mike Kennealy's 14% fell short of the 15% threshold, prompting his immediate campaign suspension. Minogue's fundraising dominance—nearly $10 million cash on hand—fuels a statewide ad campaign reaching millions of impressions, bolstering his outsider appeal as a former Abiomed CEO and combat veteran focused on affordability, audits, and tax cuts, while party leaders urge unity ahead of the primary.

markets.question: Will Virginia use a new congressional map for the 2026 United States midterm elections?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed US state uses a new congressional district map for the 2026 United States midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

To qualify, after the 2024 United States congressional elections, the listed state must have adopted a new congressional district map that is:
- Formally adopted and enacted into law by the appropriate legislative or redistricting authority;
- Not enjoined, vacated or otherwise fully struck down prior to the 2026 United States Midterm elections; and
- In effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are temporarily stayed pending appeal but later upheld will qualify if they are in effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are completely redrawn by a court, special master, or legislature before implementation will not qualify, unless the redrawn map is itself a new map used in the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If the 2026 United States midterm elections occur and no new qualifying map is in use, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the listed state and a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Which states will use new congressional maps in the midterms?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed US state uses a new congressional district map for the 2026 United States midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

To qualify, after the 2024 United States congressional elections, the listed state must have adopted a new congressional district map that is:
- Formally adopted and enacted into law by the appropriate legislative or redistricting authority;
- Not enjoined, vacated or otherwise fully struck down prior to the 2026 United States Midterm elections; and
- In effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are temporarily stayed pending appeal but later upheld will qualify if they are in effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are completely redrawn by a court, special master, or legislature before implementation will not qualify, unless the redrawn map is itself a new map used in the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If the 2026 United States midterm elections occur and no new qualifying map is in use, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mid-decade congressional redistricting efforts have accelerated ahead of the 2026 midterms, with seven states—California, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Texas, Utah, and Virginia—enacting new maps since 2025, potentially netting partisan gains like +5 Democratic seats in California and +5 Republican seats in Texas following Supreme Court clearance on April 27. The Court's April 29 ruling in Louisiana v. Callais struck down Louisiana's map as a racial gerrymander, delaying primaries and forcing a redraw, while Florida's legislature approved a new map that day awaiting Gov. DeSantis' signature. Ongoing litigation in Georgia and a Missouri Supreme Court hearing on May 14 heighten uncertainty, as traders weigh implementation risks before November elections.

markets.question: Set 1 Winner: Silva vs Mena
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Daniel Dutra da Silva and Facundo Mena in the Santos, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Silva” if Daniel Dutra da Silva wins the first set. It will resolve to “Mena” if Facundo Mena wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger match statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Santos: Daniel Dutra da Silva vs Facundo Mena Set 1 Winner

events.title: Santos: Daniel Dutra da Silva vs Facundo Mena
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Daniel Dutra da Silva and Facundo Mena in the Santos, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Daniel Dutra da Silva' if Daniel Dutra da Silva advances against Facundo Mena.

This market will resolve to 'Facundo Mena' if Facundo Mena advances against Daniel Dutra da Silva.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Santos Brasil Tennis Cup Challenger on outdoor clay, Facundo Mena enters as the slight trader consensus favorite at 50.5% implied probability for this first-round matchup against Brazilian Daniel Dutra da Silva, reflecting Mena's higher ATP ranking around 290 versus Dutra da Silva's 382, No. 4 seeding, and 5-4 head-to-head edge across nine prior meetings. markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will South Carolina use a new congressional map for the 2026 United States midterm elections?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed US state uses a new congressional district map for the 2026 United States midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

To qualify, after the 2024 United States congressional elections, the listed state must have adopted a new congressional district map that is:
- Formally adopted and enacted into law by the appropriate legislative or redistricting authority;
- Not enjoined, vacated or otherwise fully struck down prior to the 2026 United States Midterm elections; and
- In effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are temporarily stayed pending appeal but later upheld will qualify if they are in effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are completely redrawn by a court, special master, or legislature before implementation will not qualify, unless the redrawn map is itself a new map used in the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If the 2026 United States midterm elections occur and no new qualifying map is in use, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the listed state and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mid-decade congressional redistricting efforts have accelerated ahead of the 2026 midterms, with seven states—California, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Texas, Utah, and Virginia—enacting new maps since 2025, potentially netting partisan gains like +5 Democratic seats in California and +5 Republican seats in Texas following Supreme Court clearance on April 27. The Court's April 29 ruling in Louisiana v. Callais struck down Louisiana's map as a racial gerrymander, delaying primaries and forcing a redraw, while Florida's legislature approved a new map that day awaiting Gov. DeSantis' signature. Ongoing litigation in Georgia and a Missouri Supreme Court hearing on May 14 heighten uncertainty, as traders weigh implementation risks before November elections.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 3 major space weather events this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of geomagnetic storms, solar radiation storms, or radio blackouts with a severity level of 3 or greater which occur between May 3, 2026, 12:00AM ET and May 9, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A G3 event is a geomagnetic storm with a classification indicating "Strong". An S3 event is a solar radiation storm with a "Strong" classification. An R3 event is a radio blackout event with a classification indicating "Strong". More information on the space weather classification criteria can be found here: https://www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

This market may not resolve until the end of the relevant timeframe. This market will resolve according to the final number of qualifying events at the end of this market's timeframe to allow for revisions to the data. Only the initial report of an ongoing event with multiple reports will qualify, unless the rating of the event is upgraded to a qualifying level from a non-qualifying level within this market's timeframe.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve based on information from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Space Weather Prediction Center in the "ALERTS, WATCHES AND WARNINGS" table located under the heading titled "Current Space Weather Conditions" at https://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/alerts-watches-and-warnings#.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on major space weather events—defined by NOAA SWPC as G3+ geomagnetic storms, S3+ solar radiation storms, or R3+ radio blackouts—for May 3-9 remains tightly contested between zero (48%) and one (47.5%), reflecting subdued forecasts amid Solar Cycle 25's waning peak. Recent NOAA 3-day outlooks show quiet conditions with 50% odds for minor R1-R2 flares, just 10% for R3+, and no G2+ geomagnetic activity predicted beyond a possible Kp=5 (G2) peak on May 7 from a glancing CME around May 5. Late April featured isolated M-class flares and a G1 storm but no qualifiers, quieting active sunspot regions like AR4420. Swing factors include surprise X-class flares triggering R3+ blackouts or CME orientation upgrades; daily SWPC alerts and coronagraph imagery through May 9 will be pivotal for resolution.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's June 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for June 25, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 84% implied probability to no change in Banco de México's (Banxico) target overnight interbank rate at its June 2026 meeting, following the surprise 25 basis point cut to 6.75% on March 26 amid a divided Governing Board vote and despite persistent inflation above the 3% target. Early April 2026 headline CPI eased to 4.53% year-on-year from prior highs, signaling modest disinflation that supports a potential pause after the expected May 7 rate reduction priced at over 90% in parallel markets. Governor remarks on March 30 highlighted the easing cycle nearing completion, with core price pressures and rising global yields reinforcing trader caution for further adjustments. The May decision remains the pivotal catalyst ahead.

markets.question: O/U 1.5 Rounds
markets.description:
Each market will resolve to “Over” if the fight between Yaroslav Amosov and Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, lasts beyond the listed round threshold.

Each market will resolve to “Under” if the fight between Yaroslav Amosov and Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, does not last beyond the listed round threshold.

For example:

“Over 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight continues past the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

“Under 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight ends before the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

If the fight ends exactly at the threshold (e.g., 2:30 of Round 2 for a 1.5 line), it will resolve “50-50.”

If the bout is not scored, postponed beyond May 23, 2026, or canceled, all options will resolve “50-50.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC 328: Yaroslav Amosov vs. Joel Álvarez (Welterweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yaroslav Amosov" if Yaroslav Amosov is officially declared the winner of the fight against Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026.

It will resolve to "Joel Álvarez" if Joel Álvarez is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yaroslav Amosov enters this welterweight prelim at UFC 328 on May 9 in Newark with momentum from his dominant UFC debut in December 2025, submitting veteran Neil Magny via first-round anaconda choke to affirm his elite grappling and wrestling pedigree as a former Bellator champion (29-1 MMA, 1-0 UFC). Joel Álvarez (23-3 MMA, 8-2 UFC), a 6'3" striker with submission threats, rides a three-fight win streak capped by unanimous decision over Vicente Luque in October 2025, marking his successful welterweight transition from lightweight. Traders weigh Amosov's ground control against Álvarez's reach and knockout power (recent KO/TKO wins over Drakkar Klose and Elves Brener), with no reported injuries ahead of Friday's weigh-ins; stylistic clash and short-notice hype since April booking define the competitive trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Fernando Tatis Jr. win the 2026 National League Hank Aaron Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 National League Hank Aaron Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Shohei Ohtani leads trader consensus at 31% implied probability for the 2026 NL Hank Aaron Award thanks to his elite early-season slash line (.854 OPS, 6 HR) paired with dominant pitching (0.60 ERA over first five starts, earning NL Pitcher of the Month for March/April), underscoring his unmatched offensive ceiling in the Dodgers' lineup. Juan Soto ranks second at 14.5% following his April 6 activation from the 10-day injured list with the Mets, where his 2025 breakout (43 HR, 38 SB) and projected 5.8 fWAR fuel optimism despite the team's slow start. Ronald Acuña Jr. sits third at 10% after a scorching hot streak halted by a Grade 1 left hamstring strain on May 2, sidelining the Braves star for weeks. Bryce Harper, Kyle Schwarber, and Francisco Lindor follow with power-hitting pedigrees and favorable matchups in Phillies and Mets lineups, while the market reflects a competitive field with long season volatility ahead.

markets.question: Will gas hit (Low) $4.25 by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and May 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

eventMetadata.context_description: US retail regular gasoline prices have climbed to a national average of $4.46 per gallon as of May 4, 2026—up nearly 30 cents in the past week—primarily driven by WTI crude oil surging past $106 per barrel amid escalating Middle East geopolitical tensions and spring demand acceleration. Recent EIA data reveals significant gasoline stock draws and a 11% jump in consumption to multi-year highs, exacerbating supply tightness as refineries operate near capacity ahead of summer driving season. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects this volatility, with Memorial Day weekend travel and weekly EIA petroleum status reports (next due May 8) as key catalysts that could push prices toward seasonal peaks or trigger pullbacks if crude stabilizes or Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases materialize.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 73% implied probability against the U.S. enacting a comprehensive AI safety bill before 2027, driven by the Trump administration's March 2026 National Policy Framework urging Congress to prioritize light-touch regulation, innovation leadership, and preemption of state laws over broad safety mandates. Despite numerous bills introduced in the 119th Congress—such as the American Artificial Intelligence Leadership Act and AI Accountability Act—none have advanced toward passage amid narrow Republican majorities in the House (217-212) and Senate. Recent bipartisan efforts, like a Senate bill restricting kids' AI chatbot use announced April 28, remain narrowly focused, with 2026 midterms looming as a potential catalyst for gridlock or shift.

markets.question: Will Bert Mizusawa be the Republican nominee for Senate in Virginia?

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Virginia Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:

If no 2026 Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Retired Army Maj. Gen. Bert Mizusawa leads trader consensus at 42% implied probability for Virginia's Republican U.S. Senate primary on August 4, reflecting his top fundraising total of $192,000 through Q1 2026 and prior statewide candidacy experience that boosts name recognition among base voters valuing military credentials. David Williams trails at 19% on his 30-year Navy and Marine Corps service record, while Kim Farington commands 16% amid high Polymarket trading volume on her campaign. With no public polls released and the Republican Party of Virginia recently certifying these three qualifiers, odds hinge on early metrics like cash on hand—Mizusawa's $55,000 strongest—and candidate biographies in this competitive, low-information primary phase ahead of potential debates or endorsements.

markets.question: Will the next Google Gemini model debut at a score of at least 1480?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Gemini model released by Google has at least the specified score on the Arena.AI Leaderboard (arena.ai/leaderboard/text) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on the date after the day of the release, 12:00 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If no qualifying score for the specified model is available on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If no qualifying score becomes available by the end of the seventh day following the day of the model’s release, or if no qualifying model release occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

If multiple models are released on the same calendar date or if multiple variants of the qualifying model appear on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at the relevant check time (e.g., base, “Pro,” or “Flash”), the highest-scoring variant will be used for resolution.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at arena.ai/leaderboard/text. If this resolution source is unavailable at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If it remains unavailable through the end of the seventh day after a qualifying release, it will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Google's next Gemini model is poised for a strong Arena.AI leaderboard debut, driven by Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian's April 24 statement teasing a new version "very, very soon" backed by superior internal benchmarks. Recent leaks of Gemini 3.2 Flash—or possibly 3.5—have appeared on LM Arena, signaling pre-release testing ahead of Google I/O on May 19-20, where official Gemini updates and agentic coding demos are confirmed. Amid fierce rivalry from Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 (current Text Arena leader) and OpenAI's GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview holds fourth place at 1493 Elo, reflecting Google's sustained competitive edge in large language model capabilities and multimodal benchmarks. Traders anticipate rapid post-launch votes could propel it higher, per historical patterns.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - May 4, 4PM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 27.5% implied probability to repeat as contenders after bolstering their core with three-time All-Star Satou Sabally in free agency alongside re-signed stars Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu, and Jonquel Jones, creating unmatched frontcourt depth and scoring versatility that propelled them atop offseason power rankings. Indiana Fever sit close at 16.5% on Caitlin Clark's projected MVP-caliber leap, the healthy trio of Clark, Aliyah Boston, and Kelsey Mitchell, plus draft addition Raven Johnson for backcourt punch. Las Vegas Aces match at 16.0% as 2025 champions leaning on A'ja Wilson's dominance and Jewell Loyd's re-signing, while Atlanta Dream's 12.5% reflects A- offseason grades via roster upgrades enhancing Rhyne Howard's supporting cast. Minnesota Lynx round out top tier at 8.0% with Napheesa Collier anchoring a proven playoff core amid a wide-open 15-team field featuring expansion squads.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors SpaceX achieving a closing IPO market cap exceeding $2 trillion at 55% implied probability, propelled by the company's April 1 confidential SEC filing signaling a potential June listing that could raise up to $75 billion—the largest ever. This reflects explosive private valuation growth from $800 billion in a December 2025 tender offer to $1.48 trillion in April secondary trades, fueled by Starlink's satellite constellation expansion, reusable rocket dominance, and a reported xAI merger boosting AI-space synergies. Lower brackets trail as traders anticipate premium public market multiples amid S&P 500 fast-track inclusion proposals, though execution risks like regulatory reviews or volatility loom ahead of an expected late-May public filing and mid-June pricing.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro as the frontrunner (63%) to remain Venezuela's head of state through December 31, 2026, reflecting uncertainty over formal succession following his January 2026 U.S. capture and ongoing U.S. court proceedings on drug trafficking charges. Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in as interim president by the Supreme Court and military, holds 24% implied probability amid her recent military reshuffles and stabilizing signals like U.S. commercial flights resuming May 1 and oil firms engaging Caracas. Opposition figures like María Corina Machado (8%) and Edmundo González lag due to institutional barriers, barred candidacies, and no election timeline despite Machado's April calls for swift polls; UN recognition of Maduro persists absent verified transition.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 40-44 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a low volume of posts from the official @khamenei_ir X account—archival statements from the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei—at under 5 (56.5%) or 5-9 (41.5%) during May 1-8, driven by its prolonged silence since the April 13 post amid the leadership transition following his February 28 martyrdom in US-Israeli strikes. Recent US intelligence reports detailing severe injuries to successor Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, including limited official access, have fueled expectations of subdued activity, contrasting the new @MKhamenei_ir account's May 1 cluster of nine posts marking Workers' and Teachers' Days. Earlier April markets resolved around 10-14 posts, but three weeks without updates signal trader caution on resumption.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the third-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent State Duma polls from April 2026 reflect trader uncertainty in the tight contest for third place behind United Russia's projected dominance, with New People (NL) at 35% implied probability edging Communist Party (KPRF) at 32% and Liberal Democratic Party (LDPR) at 28.5%. WCIOM surveys show NL surging to 13-17%—its highest yet—bolstered by March leadership approvals positioning it as a fresh alternative amid LDPR's post-Zhirinovsky stagnation and KPRF's stable but aging base around 11%. FOM polls, however, favor LDPR at 13% over NL's 8%, highlighting pollster discrepancies and administrative resource gaps in Russia's mixed proportional-single member district system. Separation could arise from upcoming party primaries, candidate lists, or economic pressures shifting protest votes before the September 18-20 election.

Recent AFC champ New England Patriots (9%) risk regression on a tougher first-place schedule post-Super Bowl loss, as do runner-up Denver Broncos (7.5%); Houston Texans (10%) and Los Angeles Chargers (8.5%) leverage young QBs C.J. Stroud and Justin Herbert amid widespread roster continuity and wild-card volatility keeping probabilities competitive under 15%.

markets.question: Exact Score: AS Roma 1 - 0 ACF Fiorentina?
markets.description: In the upcoming Serie A game between AS Roma and ACF Fiorentina, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 2:45 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the AS Roma vs. ACF Fiorentina match originally scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 2:45 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.
